## Root Cause Summary

Actual Behavior : 

Active 8 cores frequency operating at 2800MHz instead of TRL Binbucket-0 frequency 3200Mhz while remaining 182 cores are in C1e state

Expected Behavior: 

Expecting active core frequency should match with TRL bin bucket while remaining cores are limiting to C1e.

Isolation:

1.  Active 8 cores frequency operating at 3200MHz while keeping remaining idle cores in C6 state.

Idle Cores in C1e state:

 

IDLE cores in C6 State:

### Description
Actual Behavior : 

Active 8 cores frequency operating at 2800MHz instead of TRL Binbucket-0 frequency 3200Mhz while remaining 182 cores are in C1e state

Expected Behavior: 

Expecting active core frequency should match with TRL bin bucket while remaining cores are limiting to C1e.

### Comments (latest)
++++1667410187 thangama
<p>Updates from central sighting, marking it as future based on the below comments to track.</p><p><br /></p><p><img src="https://hsdes.intel.com/rest/binary/16029391035" style="width: 1658.22px;" /><br /></p>

++++1667410188 thangama
Automated Message: This record has been cloned. The preceding comments are originally from the parent record 16029357820.

++++1667410195 thangama
Promoted this HSD from BHS CWF to DMR for Enhancement. Current Issue: When transitioning 8 cores from C1e to active C0 state (with workload), these cores operate at ACT frequency instead of their designated TRL Bin bucket frequency, while all remaining cores stay in C1e state. Enhancement Request: This issue has been identified in BHS. We require a DMR enhancement to ensure that the 8 active cores (regardless of which bin bucket they belong to) operate at their respective TRL bin bucket frequencies rather than ACT frequency, while maintaining all other cores in C1e state.
++++22611831362 mbfausto
I have pinged PM offline, this as filed is a request and NOT a sighting and should be rejected.  A proper CCB or IP ticket should be filed as an enhancement request to be decided if addressed.  There is no debug here at all.  Seems this shoudl have been filed a CWF Primecode/PM --> Primecode/PM feature request (not a sighting which can make no POR decisions here on feature requests).
++++14615297550 vwang 
This is not an issue or feature on DMR. If we really need this,  we need to have a new design on XEON. ﻿[26ww15.1] the inability for cores to reach higher turbo buckets unless some are in C6 is a known and expected behavior for Xeon TRL, not a bug or defect. This behavior is documented and has been recognized as a competitive gap. The team discussed the process for handling such requests, agreeing that if customers desire a change, it should be filed as a feature enhancement through the appropriate channels (e.g., IPS), rather than as a defect or bug. The current implementation blocks higher frequencies if cores are in C1E, regardless of per-core or module-level signaling.

### Conclusion
not_a_bug

hw.big_core
